assertions.title: CLI Serve Path Traversal Protection
assertions.reason: Path traversal attacks are properly blocked. The serve_static function uses canonicalize() to resolve paths and checks that the resolved path is within the serve directory. Both Unix-style (../) and Windows-style (..\) traversal attempts are rejected.

assertions.title: CLI Serve Safe CORS Defaults

evidence.description: Default server: no CORS headers for evil.com origin
Default server: no CORS headers on GET with Origin
evidence.observed: No CORS headers (correct for default behavior)

evidence.description: Start server with explicit CORS origin on port 3852
CORS-enabled server: ACAO header present for allowed origin

evidence.command: curl -X OPTIONS -H "Origin: https://spikes.sh" -H "Access-Control-Request-Method: POST" -I http://localhost:3852/spikes 2>&1 | grep access-control
evidence.observed: access-control-allow-origin: https://spikes.sh, access-control-allow-methods: GET,POST,OPTIONS, access-control-allow-headers: content-type,authorization

assertions.reason: Default CORS behavior is restrictive (same-origin only). CORS headers are only emitted when --cors-allow-origin is explicitly specified. The implementation in serve.rs correctly conditionally applies the CorsLayer only when the flag is provided.

assertions.title: Worker Modularization - Index.ts Split

evidence.expected: Separate modules for handlers, db, middleware, utils
evidence.observed: src/index.ts, src/constants.ts, src/types.ts, src/db/queries.ts, src/handlers/spikes.ts, src/handlers/shares.ts, src/handlers/subdomain.ts, src/handlers/prospects.ts, src/handlers/webhooks.ts, src/middleware/auth.ts, src/middleware/rate-limit.ts, src/utils/content.ts, src/utils/crypto.ts, src/utils/response.ts, src/utils/validation.ts
No circular imports to index.ts found

assertions.reason: The monolithic index.ts has been successfully split into logical modules. The main index.ts is now 130 lines (previously ~800 lines), with clear separation of concerns: handlers for route logic, db for database operations, middleware for cross-cutting concerns (auth, rate limiting), and utils for shared utilities.
